description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 output & agency cluster: tool/function least-privilege vs injection defense vs output execution vs structured validation, and against the shipped agent-authorization-matrix and human-approval-boundary.

cases.prompt: Lock down what our agent's tools can do — least privilege, run-as-user, and approval on the destructive ones.
cases.reason: Scoping the agent's runtime tool/function surface is this skill; prompt-injection-defender guards the content boundary, and agent-authorization-matrix governs standing agent-vs-human authority (merge/deploy), not per-tool runtime scopes.

cases.prompt: Define which actions our AI agents may take autonomously versus which always need a named human — merging to protected branches, deploys, arming auto-merge.
cases.reason: Standing SDLC authority (what an agent may ever do autonomously, who merges) is agent-authorization-matrix; agent-tool-safety-guard scopes the runtime tools of a product agent.

cases.prompt: Our agent writes and runs Python in a sandbox. Review the code-execution and sandbox boundaries.
cases.reason: Executing agent-generated code and sandbox escape is llm-output-safety-reviewer's output-handling surface; agent-tool-safety-guard scopes named tools, not arbitrary code execution.

cases.prompt: Define the JSON schema and validation for the model's tool-call output so malformed arguments are rejected before use.
cases.reason: The schema-validation mechanism for model output is structured-output-validator; agent-tool-safety-guard composes it as the argument-validation step but owns the permission/identity model, not the schema.

cases.prompt: Set up the general policy for when work must stop and get explicit human approval before crossing high-risk boundaries.
cases.reason: The general approval-boundary policy is human-approval-boundary; this skill composes it to gate specific high-impact tools, not to define the approval mechanism itself.
